arguments.argument: The proposal to grant unrestricted access to citizens’ private data – health monitors, security cameras, and location data – is a dangerous overreach and a fundamental violation of privacy.
arguments.justification_for_type: This is a strong argument as it directly addresses the core issue of the dilemma, stating that the proposal is a dangerous overreach and a fundamental violation of privacy.
arguments.justification_for_response: Privacy is a fundamental right, but so is the right to life and health. During a public health emergency, the moral imperative to protect the greater good—preventing mass casualties and ensuring equitable access to care—overrides individual privacy concerns.

arguments.argument: While the desire to contain a public health emergency is understandable, sacrificing privacy as a solution sets a precedent that erodes the very foundations of a free society.
